summary: Strong newsletter batch — two new major model releases (Kimi K3 from Moonshot AI, Qwen-3.8 from Alibaba), AI policy signal from Demis Hassabis/DeepMind, and a directly relevant agent engineering article. Import AI 465 provides a curated round-up tying several threads together.
url_resolution_note: Redirect URLs in the checkpoint are truncated by the email ingest script (JWT tokens redacted with '...' for security). Canonical Open Substack article URLs are included where available. Beehiiv links (Qwen-3.8 newsletter) are fully encrypted and cannot be resolved without the original email body.

cross_cutting_themes.significance: Two major open-weight model releases in one day (Kimi K3, Qwen-3.8) with benchmark claims. The open-vs-closed gap is narrowing. This is a recurring wiki theme that should be actively tracked.
Moonshot AI and Alibaba both releasing competitive models. Worth monitoring for updates to existing Chinese AI ecosystem pages.
Demis Hassabis policy proposal is a notable signal for AI safety/governance coverage in the wiki.
Practical guidance on agent harness design — directly applicable to wiki's agent framework coverage.

articles_to_resolve.note: Import AI 465 contains 10 embedded redirect links (substack.com/redirect/...) that likely point to individual articles about open models, Kimi K3, and Demis Hassabis policy. These need resolution via the full email body or web scraping the newsletter page itself.
Beehiiv encrypted links from Qwen-3.8 newsletter cannot be resolved from truncated checkpoint. Need to scrape the newsletter page directly or re-process the original email.
